sections.heading: RT
sections.content: RT plays a critical adjuvant role for locoregional control, especially in the presence of high-risk features. It is also used as definitive local therapy for inoperable primary tumors or as palliative therapy for metastatic disease.

timeline.title: Adjuvant RT to Primary Tumor Site
timeline.body: Total dose: 50-60 Gy; Dose per fraction: 1.8-2 Gy (conventional) or 2.2-2.5 Gy, 3-3.5 Gy, 8 Gy (hypofractionated); Fractions: 25-33 (conventional) or 10-20, 1, 3 (hypofractionated); Schedule: Daily (conventional) or varies (hypofractionated). Indication: After excision with negative (50-56 Gy), microscopically positive (56-60 Gy), or grossly positive (60 Gy) margins.

timeline.title: Adjuvant RT to Draining Nodal Basin
timeline.body: Total dose: 50-60 Gy; Dose per fraction: 1.8-2 Gy (conventional) or 2.2-2.5 Gy, 3-3.5 Gy, 8 Gy (hypofractionated); Fractions: 25-33 (conventional) or 10-20, 2 (hypofractionated); Schedule: Daily (conventional) or varies (hypofractionated). Indication: After SLNB positive (50-56 Gy), after LN dissection with multiple involved nodes and/or ENE (50-60 Gy).

timeline.title: Definitive RT to Clinically Evident Primary Tumor
timeline.body: Total dose: 56-60 Gy; Dose per fraction: 1.8-2 Gy (conventional) or 2.5 Gy, 3 Gy, 4 Gy, 6-7 Gy, 8 Gy (hypofractionated); Fractions: 28-33 (conventional) or 10-20, 15-17, 10, 5, 3 (hypofractionated); Schedule: Daily (conventional) or varies (hypofractionated). Indication: For unresectable primary tumor, surgery refused, or surgery would result in significant morbidity.

timeline.title: Definitive RT to Clinically Evident Nodal Disease
timeline.body: Total dose: 46-60 Gy; Dose per fraction: 1.8-2 Gy (conventional) or 2.5 Gy, 3 Gy, 6-7 Gy (hypofractionated); Fractions: 23-33 (conventional) or 20, 15-17, 5 (hypofractionated); Schedule: Daily (conventional) or varies (hypofractionated). Indication: For clinically evident lymphadenopathy (60 Gy), or clinically node-negative but at risk for subclinical disease (46-50 Gy).
